regulatory_references.name: PMI PMBOK
regulatory_references.relevance: Standard framework for project management office design

regulatory_references.name: P3O
regulatory_references.relevance: AXELOS framework for PMO design and governance

regulatory_references.name: MSP
regulatory_references.relevance: Programme management framework for operating model transformation

compliance_patterns.description: PMO design must align with organizational governance and decision-making frameworks
Operating model changes require comprehensive documentation and stakeholder sign-off

evaluator_insights.insight: PMO RFPs value practical implementation experience over theoretical models
evaluator_insights.implication: Proposals must demonstrate PMO setups that achieved measurable project delivery improvement
evaluator_insights.recommended_action: Include PMO maturity metrics: on-time delivery %, budget variance reduction, resource utilization improvement

evaluator_insights.insight: Operating model RFPs require deep understanding of organizational culture
evaluator_insights.implication: Cookie-cutter operating models score poorly — customization signals are critical
evaluator_insights.recommended_action: Include organizational assessment phase and show adaptable model variants

methodology_patterns.when_to_use: Holistic operating model design covering all organizational dimensions
Organizational alignment assessment during operating model transformation

forbidden_assumptions: Do NOT assume the client wants a centralized PMO — assess which PMO model fits their culture
Do NOT assume existing project management processes are absent — assess maturity first
Do NOT propose a one-size-fits-all operating model — customization based on organizational context is essential
